Brassica wahlenbergii is a species of mustard plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Brassica wahlenbergii include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and other sulfur-containing compounds that may contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Brassica wahlenbergii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to make various remedies, such as tinctures and decoctions.
